title: [FIRRTL] infer width (cvt)
body:
The following FIRRTL program:

```python
circuit top_mod :
  module top_mod :
    output tmp891: SInt<1>
    wire tmp871: UInt
    tmp871 <= head(SInt<18>(42), 0)
    tmp891 <= cvt(tmp871)
```

Compiled with `firtool a_top_mod.fir --lower-to-hw --expand-whens --infer-widths --lowering-options=noAlwaysFF --verilog -o=a_top_mod_new.v` fails with the following error:

```
a_top_mod.fir:6:15: error: 'firrtl.cvt' op result type should be '!firrtl.sint'
    tmp891 <= cvt(tmp871)
              ^
a_top_mod.fir:6:15: note: see current operation: %1 = "firrtl.cvt"(%tmp871) : (!firrtl.uint) -> !firrtl.sint<1>
```

Compiled with `firrtl-1.5-SNAPSHOT` produces this Verilog:

```verilog
module top_mod(
  output  tmp891
);
  wire [1:0] _GEN_0 = {1'b0,$signed(1'h0)};
  assign tmp891 = _GEN_0[0];
endmodule
```
